understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete the current level by navigating the tile grid to reach the goal gem tile marked `+` without dying, using up to 2 attempts per level and a limited number of turns per attempt.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: On each turn I could MOVE_LEFT or MOVE_RIGHT (horizontal only). After a horizontal move, gravity automatically carried the character in the current gravity direction until landing on a solid/support tile, hitting a hazard, or reaching the goal. CLICK could be used on clickable/breakable tiles (e.g., `x`) at a specified [x,y] to remove them and open paths; clicks did not move the player. Landing on spike tiles (`u`/`v`) ended the run immediately. UNDO would revert the previous action if needed. The structured previews (left_landing/right_landing and corridor_scan) indicate where a move will ultimately land after gravity, helping choose safe passage columns to change vertical sections.
